ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Reserve And Blocked Account For Child Of Sean Watts
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Continued To Jun-10-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Reserve And Blocked Account For Child Of Sean Watts
Off Calendar May-20-2014 Continued To Jun-03-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Caldwell for Linda Neal.

Status of the reserve, etc. was continued to June 3, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Caldwell to file an ex parte petition to amend distribution to Sean Watts to provide that the funds be deposited into a blocked account for his unborn children.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Reserve And Blocked Account For Child Of Sean Watts
Off Calendar May-06-2014 Continued To May-20-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-96-266340 The Estate of Nicie Lee Bright and PES-00-280962 The Estate of Bessie Ann Jackson.

Appearances: Karen Caldwell for and with Linda Neal. Dennis Vann for Kelly Dearman and Linda Harrington.

Ms. Caldwell provided endorsed copies of the receipts on distribution of the pecuniary gifts, stated that she can address the issue of the difference in the amount of interest paid (6% versus 7%) and also provided endorsed copies of the receipts for preliminary distributions to the residual Beneficiaries. Ms. Caldwell also stated that Attorney Mary Jane Gordon made an agreement to not participate in the statutory fees, that Attorney John Lyons is now deceased, that Mr. Lyons Wife agreed to waive fees, that the other residual Beneficiaries signed waivers of notice of the declaration requesting instructions and that waivers of the accounting were filed today. Furthermore, Ms. Caldwell stated that they paid the principal of Ms. Dearman and Ms. Harrington's fees, that there is a question about Ms. Harrington's request of 10% interest and that only one check was sent to Ms. Dearman. Mr. Vann stated that is is his understanding that fees were paid to Ms. Harrington and that Ms. Dearman's fees were not. In addition, Ms. Caldwell stated that the payment to Ms. Dillard was for property taxes and repairs to the stairs for the Courtland Street property, and that she filed the receipts and has consents from all of the Beneficiaries. The Parties stepped out of the courtroom to discuss what is owed to Ms. Dearman and Ms. Harrington. Status of the reserve and blocked account, and the petition for final distribution were continued to May 20, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to discuss resolution.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Bui. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Oct-01-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Caldwell for Linda Neal. George Shewl (Interested Party) in propria persona.

Mr. Shewl presented the Court with a brief background as to Bessie Jackson and Nicie Lee Bright, and expressed his concerns with the handling of the estate by Ms. Neal. Ms. Neal provided the Court with a brief history regarding Bessie Jackson, stated Mr. Neal was not an heir of Nicie Lee Bright, that he was not a beneficiary, and that all the beneficiaries had signed off to sell the property. The Court informed Mr. Shewl that it was its' belief that he was late, that he didn't have standing, and that if he wished to contest anything he should contact a lawyer. The Court confirmed the tentative ruling.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.
